frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Sach bolun, maa ke haath ke khane ki baat hi alag hoti hai, usmein sirf taste nahi, poora ghar basa hota hai. Aajkal ki bahuen sab kuch jaldi mein kar leti hain, par pyaar se roz garam roti banana bhi ghar ko ghar banata hai. Hamari generation ne isi mein apna sukh dekha, tabhi bachche bhi maa se jude rehte the.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it idealizes a woman’s domestic labor and self-sacrifice as the essence of motherhood and home, while implicitly judging daughters-in-law who do not perform this standard. It encodes nostalgic sentimentalization, labor erasure, and comparison between mothers and younger women.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 24 years. A woman who worked for six years before stopping when her first child was born. You are at a family function sitting with three other women your age. One woman mentions that her daughter in law has just gone back to work full time when her child is only eight months old. The conversation opens up. You are giving your opinion on women who return to work this early and what it means for the child and what you think women of this generation have confused about what ambition is supposed to look like and what you gave up and whether you regret it.
